column1: # Reconciler skip-list — env/package pairs the reconciler should NOT keep retrying.
# aarch.science's gaps differ from aarchbio's (DESIGN D2/D3). aarchbio's unit is a
# single bioconda tool that may have "no arm64 build". Here the unit is a curated
# ENV, and a gap is a package we'd want in an env that EITHER won't solve on arm64
# conda-forge OR solves-but-won't-assemble/import on arm64 (the D3 failure mode —
# the subtle one). When such a package blocks an env, record it here so the daily
# reconciler stops re-trying a known dead-end, and document it in GAPS.md.
# Two tiers (mirrors aarchbio):
#   wontfix  — genuine dead-end; never retry (only a human edit removes it).
#   stuck    — real chance of an upstream fix (conda-forge feedstock re-enables
#              arm64, an ABI bug is fixed); skip the DAILY run but re-check every
#              <revisit_days>.
# Columns (tab-separated):  package  tier  revisit_days  reason
# (revisit_days is ignored for wontfix; use 0. `package` is the conda-forge package
#  name; prefix with `env:` to skip a whole env spec instead.)
# Edit this file to add/remove entries. The reconciler reads it each run.
# STATUS: EMPTY. As of 2026-06, every package probed for the planned envs
# (geospatial, earth-observation, geo-ml, climate, lidar/point-cloud) solves clean
# on conda-forge linux-aarch64 — there are no known hard arm64 gaps in the curated
# science head. The mechanism exists so that when a gap DOES appear (most likely a
# D3 assemble-not-solve failure on a future package), we have a place to record it
# instead of looping on it forever. See GAPS.md.
# --- wontfix: genuine dead-ends (none yet) ---
# --- stuck: arm64 blocked with a real chance of upstream fix (none yet) ---
